summary: Do not ship. P-2, P-3, and the committed RED witness now look sound, but the new cohort wait can freeze reconciliation indefinitely. No materially simpler safe root fix was found.

findings.title: Unbounded cohort wait can strand a committed object and halt GC
findings.body: The claimed `< gc_grace` bound is false. `upload_timeout` only drops the caller future; the plan deliberately moves `PinGuard` into a non-abortable `spawn_blocking` closure and retains it through write, rename, and fsync. A queued or stuck filesystem operation can therefore keep a cohort member live indefinitely. Because GC has already moved X to its grave, a stall after pointer rename leaves a real pointer returning 404 indefinitely, while `pass_lock` prevents every subsequent recovery/GC pass. T-C3 and T-B2 always release their hooks, so neither acceptance witness covers this failure.
findings.recommendation: Specify bounded fail-closed settlement: notify and restore immediately once `landed` becomes true; otherwise time out a nonterminal cohort, restore or preserve the grave safely, release the pass, and emit an observable error. Add deterministic tests for never-released pins both before and after pointer rename, and characterize the resulting degraded-path GC deferral.

next_steps: Revise the cohort timeout and recovery contract plus acceptance tests.
Run the approved scoped plan re-review again without executing machine-owned GREEN tests.
